prp.objective: Create internal/git/git.go (NEW package `git`) defining the foundational `Git` struct (`dir string` + `git string`, both unexported; `git` holds the resolved binary path) with a thin, synchronous `run(args ...string) (stdout string, err error)` exec helper that builds the command as a `[]string` via `exec.Command(g.git, args...)` with `cmd.Dir=g.dir` (NEVER `sh -c`, per PRD §19), captures BOTH stdout and stderr into `bytes.Buffer`s (leaving `cmd.Env=nil` to inherit, `cmd.Stdin=nil`), and on a non-zero exit returns a TYPED `*git.ExitError{Args []string, Code int, Stderr string}` (extracting Code via `errors.As(err, &ee)` into the stdlib `*exec.ExitError` + `ee.ExitCode()`, and Stderr from OUR buffer because `(*exec.ExitError).Stderr` is empty when cmd.Stderr is set), plus a constructor `func New(dir string) (*Git, error)` that fail-fast resolves the git binary via `exec.LookPath("git")` (no go-git — PRD §22.3). Create internal/git/git_test.go (`package git`, white-box, stdlib `testing` ONLY) proving against the REAL git binary (git 2.54.0): `run("version")` stdout Contains "git version"; `run("rev-parse","--git-dir")` in an inline-`git init`'d temp dir returns ".git"; `run("rev-parse","--git-dir")` in a plain non-repo temp dir returns a `*git.ExitError` whose Code==128 and Stderr Contains "not a git repository"; plus `New("/nonexistent-dir-for-lookpath")`... `New` LookPath success + a typed-error `errors.As` check. DOCS impact: Mode A — godoc on `Git`/`New`/`run`/`ExitError` citing PRD §22.3/§19 and decisions.md §2.
prp.context:
# PRP — P1.M3.T1.S1: internal/git/git.go — Git wrapper + run(args) exec helper

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Build the foundational `internal/git` package's keystone — a `Git` type bound to one working directory plus a low-level `run(args)` exec helper that shells out to the REAL git binary (stdlib `os/exec`, no go-git, no `sh -c`). This `run` is the single seam every higher-level plumbing method (RevParseHEAD/WriteTree/CommitTree/UpdateRefCAS — M3.T2; StagedDiff — M3.T3; CommitCount/RecentMessages/RecentSubjects/HasStagedChanges/AddAll — M3.T4) and the temp-repo test harness (M3.T1.S2) will call. Getting the exec wrapping, typed error, and binary resolution right here unblocks all of milestone M3.

**Deliverable**: Two NEW files in a NEW `internal/git` package:
- `internal/git/git.go` — `// Package git` doc comment + the `Git` struct (unexported `dir`, `git` fields) + constructor `func New(dir string) (*Git, error)` (LookPath) + `func (g *Git) run(args ...string) (string, error)` (exec wrapper) + the exported `ExitError` type (`Args`, `Code`, `Stderr`) + its `Error()` method.
- `internal/git/git_test.go` — `package git` (white-box), stdlib `testing` ONLY, drives the REAL git binary (no mocks of git), one behavior per `Test*` function.

**Success Definition**: `go test ./internal/git/` passes against the real git binary (git 2.54.0); `New` resolves git via LookPath; `run("version")` returns stdout containing "git version"; `run("rev-parse","--git-dir")` in an init'd temp dir returns ".git"; a git command in a non-repo temp dir returns a `*git.ExitError` (Code 128, Stderr contains "not a git repository") assertable via `errors.As`. `go build ./internal/git/`, `go vet ./internal/git/` clean; `gofmt -l internal/git/` empty; `go test ./...` whole-module green. stdlib-only imports (`bytes`, `errors`, `fmt`, `os/exec`, `strings`); no go.mod/go.sum change; main.go/Makefile/ui/provider untouched; no import cycle.

## Why

- **The `internal/git` package's keystone.** Every downstream git method (T2/T3/T4) calls `g.run(...)` from the same package, and the test harness (S2) builds temp repos and drives them through `Git`/`run`. This is the FIRST file of `package git` — it owns the package doc and the exec contract everyone else inherits.
- **Shells out to real git, NOT go-git (PRD §22.3).** go-git adds a huge dependency and re-implements plumbing we trust the real binary for. Shelling out matches the proven `commit-pi` reference and guarantees identical semantics to the user's git. `run` is the literal `exec.Command("git", ...)` seam.
- **No `sh -c` (PRD §19).** Commands are a `[]string` run via `exec.Command` directly, never through a shell. `run(args ...string)` enforces this structurally — there is no code path that interpolates args into a shell string, so the entire class of shell-injection bugs is eliminated for every git call.
- **Typed `*ExitError` for clear diagnostics + routing.** A non-zero git exit is the normal signal for "not a git repository", "conflict in index", "HEAD moved" (CAS failure), etc. A typed error carrying `Args`, `Code`, and `Stderr` lets downstream callers (and tests) `errors.As` into it and route precisely — mirroring the sibling `provider.AgentError` pattern.
- **Fail-fast binary resolution via LookPath.** `New` resolves git once at construction so a missing binary surfaces as a clear error up front (at repo-open time), not as an opaque failure deep inside the first plumbing call.

## What (technical requirements)

1. NEW file `internal/git/git.go`, NEW `package git`. It MUST carry the file-level `// Package git ...` doc comment (it is the first/primary file of the package; sibling files plumbing.go/diff.go/log.go/stage.go added by later tasks, and gittestutil_test.go from S2, use a plain `package git` line — mirror how internal/ui/exitcode.go and internal/provider/manifest.go own their package docs).
2. **`Git` struct** — `type Git struct { dir string; git string }`, BOTH fields unexported. `dir` = the working directory passed to every `cmd.Dir`. `git` = the resolved path to the git binary (from `exec.LookPath` in `New`; default resolves to whatever "git" is on PATH). godoc on the struct citing PRD §22.3 (shell out to real git) and decisions.md §2 (the Git wrapper interface).
3. **Constructor** `func New(dir string) (*Git, error)`: call `exec.LookPath("git")`; on error return a wrapped `fmt.Errorf` (e.g. `"git: git binary not found in PATH: %w"`); on success return `&Git{dir: dir, git: path}`. godoc cites the fail-fast resolution intent. `dir==""` is allowed (means inherit the stagehand process cwd, matching executor.go's `Dir` semantics — do not reject it).
4. **`run` method** `func (g *Git) run(args ...string) (string, error)`: 
   - `cmd := exec.Command(g.git, args...)` (the resolved absolute path has a separator so exec.Command skips re-LookPath; NEVER build a shell string — §19).
   - `cmd.Dir = g.dir`.
   - `var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer; cmd.Stdout = &stdout; cmd.Stderr = &stderr`.
   - Leave `cmd.Env = nil` (child inherits the process env — git needs PATH/HOME/GIT_*; no `os.Environ()` call needed) and `cmd.Stdin = nil` (os/exec connects to /dev/null). Do NOT set `SysProcAttr`/`Setpgid` (that is the provider Executor's process-group concern for agent subprocesses; git plumbing is synchronous).
   - `if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil { ... }`. Inside: `var ee *exec.ExitError` (the STDLIB type, QUALIFIED); `if errors.As(err, &ee) { return "", &ExitError{Args: args, Code: ee.ExitCode(), Stderr: stderr.String()} }` — read Stderr from OUR buffer (see gotcha). Else (failed to start — binary gone, permission): `return "", fmt.Errorf("git %v: %w", args, err)`.
   - On `err == nil`: `return stdout.String(), nil` (RAW captured stdout, trailing newline included — callers like RevParseHEAD trim as needed; the version test uses `strings.Contains`).
   - godoc on `run` cites PRD §19 (no sh -c), §22.3 (real git), and the `*ExitError` contract.
5. **`ExitError` type** — `type ExitError struct { Args []string; Code int; Stderr string }` (all exported fields), godoc citing it is returned for non-zero git exits. `func (e *ExitError) Error() string` returns a one-line summary, e.g. `fmt.Sprintf("git exited %d (%s): %s", e.Code, strings.Join(e.Args, " "), strings.TrimSpace(e.Stderr))`. (See Naming-collision gotcha: `ExitError` is OUR type; the stdlib one is referenced as `exec.ExitError`.)
6. NEW file `internal/git/git_test.go`, `package git` (white-box — it MUST call the unexported `run` directly, like internal/provider/executor_test.go is white-box), stdlib `testing` ONLY (do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum). Drive the REAL git binary; create temp dirs inline with `os/exec` (do NOT import the S2 harness — it does not exist yet). Cases (see Implementation Blueprint for exact code):
   - `TestNew_ResolvesGit` — `New("")` (or `New(t.TempDir())`) succeeds; assert `g != nil` and `g.git != ""` and that it is an absolute-ish resolved path.
   - `TestNew_MissingBinary` — temporarily force LookPath failure (e.g. `t.Setenv("PATH", "")` then `exec.LookPath`-style) and assert `New` returns a non-nil error; OR assert the error path with a helper. (Keep it robust: the host always has git, so test the error branch by emptying PATH within the test — `t.Setenv("PATH", "")`.)
   - `TestRun_Version` — `g, _ := New(t.TempDir()); out, err := g.run("version")`; `err == nil`; `strings.Contains(out, "git version")`.
   - `TestRun_RevParseGitDirInRepo` — create temp dir, `git init -q` it inline via `exec.Command("git","init","-q").Run()` with `cmd.Dir=dir`; `g, _ := New(dir)`; `out, err := g.run("rev-parse","--git-dir")`; `err==nil`; `strings.TrimSpace(out)==".git"`.
   - `TestRun_NonRepoIsExitError` — `g, _ := New(t.TempDir())` (no init); `_, err := g.run("rev-parse","--git-dir")`; `var ee *ExitError`; `errors.As(err, &ee)` true; `ee.Code == 128`; `strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "not a git repository")`.
   - `TestRun_ExitErrorFields` — (optional combined) assert `ee.Args` equals the passed `[]string`.
7. Respect the dependency boundary: git_test.go must NOT import or use any S2 harness type (gittestutil); it creates its minimal `git init` inline. `run` stays UNEXPORTED (lowercase) — only same-package callers and white-box tests reach it, which is the contract.

### Success Criteria
- [ ] `internal/git/git.go` exists as the first file of a NEW `package git` with a file-level `// Package git` doc comment citing PRD §22.3/§19/decisions.md §2; imports only stdlib (`bytes`, `errors`, `fmt`, `os/exec`, `strings`); no go-git, no cobra/pflag/go-toml.
- [ ] `Git` struct has unexported `dir` and `git` fields with godoc; `New(dir string) (*Git, error)` resolves git via `exec.LookPath("git")` (fail-fast error if absent) and returns `&Git{dir, path}`; `dir==""` is accepted.
- [ ] `run(args ...string) (string, error)` builds `exec.Command(g.git, args...)` (no `sh -c`), sets `cmd.Dir=g.dir`, captures stdout+stderr into buffers, leaves `cmd.Env`/`cmd.Stdin` nil (inherit), and on non-zero exit returns `*ExitError{Args, Code, Stderr}` (Code from stdlib `*exec.ExitError` via `errors.As`, Stderr from our buffer); on start-failure returns a wrapped non-typed error; on success returns raw stdout.
- [ ] `ExitError{Args []string; Code int; Stderr string}` is exported with godoc and an `Error() string` method.
- [ ] `internal/git/git_test.go` (white-box `package git`, stdlib testing) passes against the REAL git binary: version contains "git version"; rev-parse --git-dir in an init'd temp dir returns ".git"; rev-parse --git-dir in a non-repo temp dir returns a `*ExitError` with Code 128 and Stderr containing "not a git repository" (errors.As); `New` error branch covered.
- [ ] `go test ./internal/git/` and `go test ./...` exit 0; `go build ./internal/git/` and `go vet ./internal/git/` clean; `gofmt -l internal/git/` empty.
- [ ] Mode A docs satisfied: godoc on `Git`/`New`/`run`/`ExitError` cites PRD §22.3/§19 and decisions.md §2. No README/docs created; main.go/Makefile/go.mod/go.sum/internal/ui/internal/provider untouched; no `go mod tidy`; the `// Package git` doc comment appears exactly once (in git.go).

## All Needed Context

### Documentation & References

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data models / structure
```go
package git 
import (
    "bytes"
    "errors"
    "fmt"
    "os/exec"
    "strings"
)
type Git struct {
    dir string 
    git string 
}
type ExitError struct {
    Args   []string
    Code   int
    Stderr string
}
func (e *ExitError) Error() string {
    return fmt.Sprintf("git exited %d (%s): %s", e.Code, strings.Join(e.Args, " "), strings.TrimSpace(e.Stderr))
}

```

### Implementation Tasks (ordered by dependencies)
```yaml
Task 1: CREATE internal/git/git.go
  - NEW `package git`; FILE-LEVEL `// Package git ...` doc comment citing PRD §22.3 (shell out to real git, no go-git), §19 (no sh -c), and decisions.md §2 (the Git wrapper interface). git.go OWNS the package doc.
  - IMPORTS: stdlib only — bytes, errors, fmt, os/exec, strings. NO go-git, NO cobra/pflag/go-toml.
  - DEFINE `type Git struct { dir string; git string }` (both unexported) with godoc citing §22.3/decisions.md §2.
  - DEFINE exported `type ExitError struct { Args []string; Code int; Stderr string }` with godoc; `func (e *ExitError) Error() string` one-liner (Code + joined Args + trimmed Stderr).
  - IMPLEMENT `func New(dir string) (*Git, error)`: `path, err := exec.LookPath("git"); if err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("git: git binary not found in PATH: %w", err) }; return &Git{dir: dir, git: path}, nil`. godoc cites fail-fast resolution. Accept dir=="".
  - IMPLEMENT `func (g *Git) run(args ...string) (string, error)`: `cmd := exec.Command(g.git, args...)` (no sh -c); `cmd.Dir = g.dir`; `var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer; cmd.Stdout=&stdout; cmd.Stderr=&stderr`; leave `cmd.Env=nil`, `cmd.Stdin=nil` (NO SysProcAttr). `if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil { var ee *exec.ExitError; if errors.As(err,&ee) { return "", &ExitError{Args: args, Code: ee.ExitCode(), Stderr: stderr.String()} }; return "", fmt.Errorf("git %v: %w", args, err) }; return stdout.String(), nil` (RAW stdout). godoc cites §19/§22.3 + the *ExitError contract.
  - NAMING: exported Git, New, ExitError; unexported dir, git, run. FOLLOW internal/provider/executor.go doc density + errors.As pattern.
Task 2: CREATE internal/git/git_test.go
  - `package git` (white-box — MUST call the unexported run; mirror executor_test.go which is `package provider`); stdlib `testing` ONLY (NO go.mod change); import bytes/errors/strings/testing + os/exec for inline git init.
  - TestNew_ResolvesGit: `g, err := New(t.TempDir())`; err==nil; g!=nil; g.git != "".
  - TestNew_MissingBinary: `t.Setenv("PATH", "")`; `_, err := New(t.TempDir())`; err != nil (LookPath fails with empty PATH). (Restore is automatic via t.Setenv.)
  - TestRun_Version: `g,_ := New(t.TempDir())`; `out, err := g.run("version")`; err==nil; `strings.Contains(out, "git version")`.
  - TestRun_RevParseGitDirInRepo: dir:=t.TempDir(); inline `c:=exec.Command("git","init","-q"); c.Dir=dir; if err:=c.Run(); err!=nil { t.Fatal(err) }`; `g,_ := New(dir)`; `out, err := g.run("rev-parse","--git-dir")`; err==nil; `strings.TrimSpace(out)==".git"`.
  - TestRun_NonRepoIsExitError: `g,_ := New(t.TempDir())` (no init); `_, err := g.run("rev-parse","--git-dir")`; `var ee *ExitError`; `errors.As(err,&ee)` true; `ee.Code==128`; `strings.Contains(ee.Stderr,"not a git repository")`; `ee.Args` equals `["rev-parse","--git-dir"]`.
  - DO NOT import or use any S2 harness type (gittestutil) — create temp repos INLINE.
  - NAMING: Test{New,Run}_<Scenario>; one behavior per function; use reflect.DeepEqual for the Args slice if convenient.
Task 3: VERIFY via the validation gates IN ORDER (see Validation Gates). Fix any failure before declaring done.
  - go build ./internal/git/ → 0; go vet ./internal/git/ → clean; test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/git/)" → empty; go test ./internal/git/ → PASS; go test ./... → whole-module green.
  - Mode A docs: confirm Git/New/run/ExitError each have godoc citing §22.3/§19/decisions.md §2.
  - Confirm scope: ONLY internal/git/git.go + internal/git/git_test.go created; main.go/Makefile/go.mod/go.sum/internal/ui/internal/provider unchanged; NO README/docs; no go mod tidy; `// Package git` appears exactly once.

```

### Implementation Patterns & Key Details
```go
func (g *Git) run(args ...string) (string, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command(g.git, args...) 
    cmd.Dir = g.dir
    var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
    cmd.Stdout = &stdout
    cmd.Stderr = &stderr
    if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
        var ee *exec.ExitError 
        if errors.As(err, &ee) {
            return "", &ExitError{Args: args, Code: ee.ExitCode(), Stderr: stderr.String()}
        }
        return "", fmt.Errorf("git %v: %w", args, err) 
    }
    return stdout.String(), nil 
}
dir := t.TempDir()
c := exec.Command("git", "init", "-q")
c.Dir = dir
if err := c.Run(); err != nil { t.Fatalf("git init: %v", err) }
g, _ := New(dir)
out, err := g.run("rev-parse", "--git-dir") 

```

### Integration Points
```yaml
PACKAGE: github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/git   # this task CREATES the package
LOW-LEVEL SEAM: g.run(args ...string) (string, error) — UNEXPORTED; same-package + white-box tests only.
CONSUMERS (downstream, DO NOT implement here — they call g.run from package git):
  - internal/git/plumbing.go (M3.T2.S1): RevParseHEAD (run "rev-parse","HEAD"), WriteTree (run "write-tree"), CommitTree (run "commit-tree",[-p parent?] -m msg tree), UpdateRefCAS (run "update-ref",ref,new,expected?).
  - internal/git/diff.go (M3.T3.S1): StagedDiff -> run("diff","--cached",...).
  - internal/git/log.go (M3.T4.S1): CommitCount/RecentMessages/RecentSubjects -> run("rev-list","--count","HEAD") etc.
  - internal/git/stage.go (M3.T4.S2): HasStagedChanges/AddAll -> run("diff","--cached","--name-only") / run("add","-A").
  - internal/git/gittestutil_test.go (M3.T1.S2): the temp-repo harness — builds repos + commits and drives them via Git/run.
NOT HERE: the higher-level methods above; timeout/process-group (provider Executor); config; TOML; main wiring.
DOCS: Mode A — godoc on Git/New/run/ExitError cites PRD §22.3/§19 + decisions.md §2. Create NO README/docs (the doc surface is built up in later milestones). This PRP surfaces the item's declared DOCS line so it is not silently dropped.

```

## Validation Loop

### Level 1 — Syntax/Style/Build
- `go build ./internal/git/` → exits 0 (new package compiles).
- `go vet ./internal/git/` → clean.
- `test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/git/)"` → empty (gofmt -l always exits 0; wrap in test -z — proven idiom from P1.M1/M2 PRPs).

### Level 2 — Unit Tests (the contract verification, against the REAL git binary)
- `go test ./internal/git/` → PASS (version / rev-parse-repo / non-repo-ExitError / New error-branch).
- `go test ./...` → whole-module integrity (Makefile `test` target green; internal/ui + internal/provider still pass).

### Level 3 — (none; run() is a synchronous in-process exec helper against the real git binary already exercised in Level 2 — there is no separate service/endpoint to integration-test. The end-to-end plumbing semantics — write-tree/commit-tree/update-ref CAS — are M3.T2.S1.)

## DOCS Impact
**Mode A.** Per the work item: godoc on `Git`, `New`, `run`, and `ExitError` cites PRD §22.3 (shell out to real git, no go-git), §19 (no sh -c), and decisions.md §2 (the Git wrapper). This is the ONLY documentation this task produces (inline godoc). Do NOT create README.md, docs/, or providers/*.toml here (those surfaces are fed by later milestones). This PRP surfaces the item's declared DOCS line so it is not silently dropped.

## Anti-patterns to avoid
- ❌ Do NOT import go-git or any git library — shell out to the real `git` binary via os/exec (PRD §22.3).
- ❌ Do NOT use `sh -c`/`zsh -c` or build a shell string — `run` = `exec.Command(g.git, args...)` with a `[]string` (PRD §19).
- ❌ Do NOT read `(*exec.ExitError).Stderr` — it is empty when you attach your own `cmd.Stderr` buffer; read YOUR buffer.
- ❌ Do NOT confuse OUR `ExitError` with stdlib `exec.ExitError` — the `errors.As` target is the QUALIFIED `*exec.ExitError`; the value you return is the UNQUALIFIED `&ExitError{}`.
- ❌ Do NOT export `run` (it is lowercase in the contract) — it is the low-level same-package seam; the public surface is the T2/T3/T4 methods.
- ❌ Do NOT call `os.Environ()` or set `SysProcAttr`/`Setpgid` in `run` — env is inherited (cmd.Env nil) and process-group kill is the provider Executor's concern.
- ❌ Do NOT TrimSpace stdout inside `run` — return raw stdout; callers trim as needed (version test uses Contains).
- ❌ Do NOT import the S2 test harness in git_test.go (it does not exist yet and depends on this task) — create `git init` temp dirs inline.
- ❌ Do NOT make tests `package git_test` — they are white-box `package git` to call the unexported `run`.
- ❌ Do NOT touch main.go/Makefile/go.mod/go.sum/internal/ui/internal/provider, create README/docs, or run `go mod tidy`; the `// Package git` doc comment appears exactly once.
implementationSteps: Create `internal/git/git.go` as the FIRST file of a NEW `package git` (the directory does not exist yet — create it). Write a file-level `// Package git ...` doc comment citing PRD §22.3 (shell out to the real git binary, no go-git), §19 (no `sh -c`; commands built as `[]string`), and decisions.md §2 (the internal/git.Git wrapper interface backed by `exec.Command("git",...)`); git.go OWNS the package doc (later files plumbing.go/diff.go/log.go/stage.go and gittestutil_test.go use a plain `package git` line). Import ONLY stdlib (`bytes`, `errors`, `fmt`, `os/exec`, `strings` — NO go-git, NO cobra/pflag/go-toml). Define `type Git struct { dir string; git string }` with both fields UNEXPORTED and a godoc comment citing §22.3/decisions.md §2 (`dir` = working directory passed to every `cmd.Dir`, `""` = inherit cwd; `git` = the resolved git binary path). Define the exported `type ExitError struct { Args []string; Code int; Stderr string }` with a godoc stating it is returned for non-zero git exits, plus `func (e *ExitError) Error() string` returning a one-line summary like `fmt.Sprintf("git exited %d (%s): %s", e.Code, strings.Join(e.Args, " "), strings.TrimSpace(e.Stderr))`. Implement `func New(dir string) (*Git, error)` that does `path, err := exec.LookPath("git")`; on error returns `nil, fmt.Errorf("git: git binary not found in PATH: %w", err)` (fail-fast); on success returns `&Git{dir: dir, git: path}, nil` (accept `dir==""`). Implement `func (g *Git) run(args ...string) (string, error)`: `cmd := exec.Command(g.git, args...)` (NEVER `sh -c` — the resolved path has a separator so no re-LookPath); `cmd.Dir = g.dir`; declare `var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer` and set `cmd.Stdout=&stdout`, `cmd.Stderr=&stderr`; LEAVE `cmd.Env=nil` (child inherits PATH/HOME/GIT_*) and `cmd.Stdin=nil` (/dev/null) and do NOT set `SysProcAttr`/`Setpgid` (that is the provider Executor's concern); `if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil { var ee *exec.ExitError` (QUALIFIED stdlib type) `; if errors.As(err, &ee) { return "", &ExitError{Args: args, Code: ee.ExitCode(), Stderr: stderr.String()} }` (read Stderr from OUR buffer because `(*exec.ExitError).Stderr` is empty when cmd.Stderr is set) `; return "", fmt.Errorf("git %v: %w", args, err) }` (start-failure path); on success `return stdout.String(), nil` (RAW stdout, trailing newline included). Add godoc to `run` citing §19, §22.3, and the `*ExitError` contract. Note `run` is UNEXPORTED (lowercase) per the contract.

Create `internal/git/git_test.go` as `package git` (WHITE-BOX — it MUST call the unexported `run`, exactly like internal/provider/executor_test.go is `package provider`), using the stdlib `testing` package ONLY (do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum; import `errors`, `os/exec`, `strings`, `testing`, and `reflect` if needed). Do NOT import or use any S2 harness type — create temp repos INLINE. Cases: (1) `TestNew_ResolvesGit` — `g, err := New(t.TempDir())`; `err==nil`, `g != nil`, `g.git != ""`. (2) `TestNew_MissingBinary` — `t.Setenv("PATH", "")` then `_, err := New(t.TempDir())`; `err != nil` (LookPath fails with empty PATH; t.Setenv auto-restores). (3) `TestRun_Version` — `g, _ := New(t.TempDir())`; `out, err := g.run("version")`; `err==nil`; `strings.Contains(out, "git version")` is true. (4) `TestRun_RevParseGitDirInRepo` — `dir := t.TempDir()`; inline `c := exec.Command("git", "init", "-q"); c.Dir = dir; if err := c.Run(); err != nil { t.Fatalf("git init: %v", err) }`; `g, _ := New(dir)`; `out, err := g.run("rev-parse", "--git-dir")`; `err==nil`; `strings.TrimSpace(out) == ".git"`. (5) `TestRun_NonRepoIsExitError` — `g, _ := New(t.TempDir())` (NO git init); `_, err := g.run("rev-parse", "--git-dir")`; `var ee *ExitError`; `errors.As(err, &ee)` is true; `ee.Code == 128`; `strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "not a git repository")` is true; and `ee.Args` equals `["rev-parse", "--git-dir"]` (use reflect.DeepEqual). Use one behavior per `Test*` function.

Run the validation gates IN ORDER and fix any failure before declaring done: `go build ./internal/git/` (must compile), `go vet ./internal/git/` (clean), `test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/git/)"` (no unformatted files), `go test ./internal/git/` (PASS against the real git binary — version/rev-parse-repo/non-repo-ExitError/New-error-branch), `go test ./...` (whole-module integrity; Makefile `test` target green; internal/ui + internal/provider still pass).

DOCS impact: Mode A — confirm `Git`, `New`, `run`, and `ExitError` each carry a godoc comment citing PRD §22.3/§19 and decisions.md §2. Create NO README.md/docs/providers TOML here. Confirm scope boundaries: ONLY `internal/git/git.go` and `internal/git/git_test.go` were created (new package `git`); main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, internal/ui, and internal/provider are unchanged; only stdlib imports were added (no go-git); no `go mod tidy` was run; and the `// Package git` doc comment appears exactly once (in git.go).

successCriteria.description: `internal/git/git.go` exists as the first file of a NEW `package git` with a file-level `// Package git` doc comment citing PRD §22.3/§19 and decisions.md §2, and imports ONLY stdlib (`bytes`, `errors`, `fmt`, `os/exec`, `strings`); no go-git, cobra, pflag, or go-toml import.

The `Git` struct has two unexported fields `dir string` and `git string` with godoc citing §22.3/decisions.md §2; `New(dir string) (*Git, error)` resolves the git binary via `exec.LookPath("git")` (fail-fast wrapped error if absent) and returns `&Git{dir, path}`; `dir==""` is accepted (inherit cwd).

`run(args ...string) (string, error)` builds `exec.Command(g.git, args...)` (NEVER `sh -c`), sets `cmd.Dir=g.dir`, captures stdout+stderr into `bytes.Buffer`s, leaves `cmd.Env=nil` (inherit) and `cmd.Stdin=nil` with no `SysProcAttr`, and on a non-zero exit returns a typed `*ExitError{Args, Code, Stderr}` where Code comes from the stdlib `*exec.ExitError` via `errors.As` and Stderr is read from OUR buffer; a start failure yields a wrapped non-typed error; success returns the RAW captured stdout.

`run` is UNEXPORTED (lowercase per the contract) and reachable only by same-package callers and white-box tests; the exported `ExitError{Args []string; Code int; Stderr string}` type has godoc and an `Error() string` method.

`internal/git/git_test.go` is white-box `package git` (so it can call the unexported `run`), stdlib `testing` ONLY, and passes against the REAL git binary (git 2.54.0): `run("version")` stdout Contains "git version"; `run("rev-parse","--git-dir")` in an inline-`git init`'d temp dir returns ".git"; `run("rev-parse","--git-dir")` in a plain non-repo temp dir returns a `*ExitError` (asserted via `errors.As`) with Code==128 and Stderr containing "not a git repository"; and `New` returns a non-nil error when git is absent from PATH.
`go test ./internal/git/` exits 0 (PASS) and `go test ./...` exits 0 (whole-module integrity; Makefile `test` target green; internal/ui + internal/provider tests unaffected).
`go build ./internal/git/` and `go vet ./internal/git/` are clean, and `gofmt -l internal/git/` produces no output.

Scope boundaries respected: only internal/git/git.go and internal/git/git_test.go created; git_test.go creates temp repos INLINE (does not import the not-yet-existing S2 harness); main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, internal/ui, and internal/provider unchanged; no README/docs/providers TOML created; no `go mod tidy`; the `// Package git` doc comment appears exactly once.

references: PRD.md §22.3 (lines 1307-1312) — THE mandate: 'No git library dependency. Stagehand shells out to the real git binary (matching commit-pi). go-git is tempting but adds a large dependency... Shelling out is simpler, matches the reference implementation, and guarantees identical semantics.' file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §19 (lines 1200-1202) — THE security mandate: 'No shell interpolation. Commands are built as []string and run via exec.Command directly, never via sh -c / zsh -c.' run() = exec.Command(g.git, args...); no sh -c path. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §20.1 layer 2 (lines 1212-1217) — test posture: 'Unit — git wrapper, with a real git binary. Each internal/git/* test creates a temp directory, git init, stages known content...' (tests use the REAL git binary, no mocks). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md §2 — the internal/git.Git interface is 'Backed by exec.Command("git", ...). Tested with a temp repo + real git binary.' and lists the downstream methods (RevParseHEAD/WriteTree/CommitTree/UpdateRefCAS/HasStagedChanges/AddAll/StagedDiff/CommitCount/RecentMessages/RecentSubjects) that consume run().
plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/system_context.md §2 — environment: git 2.54.0 present, go 1.26.4 (>=1.22 required); validated host for all test assertions.

internal/provider/executor.go — THE in-repo os/exec reference: exec.Command(name,args...), cmd.Dir, bytes.Buffer stdout/stderr, a typed error struct (*AgentError), exit-code extraction via errors.As(err,&ee)+ee.ExitCode() where ee is *exec.ExitError, tail-trim excerpt helper, package-doc density. git.run is a SIMPLER sibling (no timeout, no Setpgid, no stdin feed).

internal/provider/executor_test.go — THE in-repo test reference: white-box package-provider tests, stdlib testing only, drive REAL host binaries, one behavior per Test*, errors.As assertions, t.TempDir()+filepath.EvalSymlinks for cwd checks.
internal/ui/exitcode.go — package-doc ownership precedent: git.go OWNS the '// Package git' doc comment exactly as exitcode.go owns '// Package ui'; sibling files use plain 'package git'.
os/exec.Command — builds the Cmd; when name is an absolute resolved path it skips re-LookPath; cmd.Dir/Stdout/Stderr/Env/Stdin fields. https://pkg.go.dev/os/exec#Command
os/exec.LookPath — resolves 'git' in $PATH; used by New for fail-fast resolution; returns error if absent. https://pkg.go.dev/os/exec#LookPath
os/exec.Cmd.Run — starts+waits; returns nil on exit 0, *exec.ExitError on non-zero exit, other error on start failure. https://pkg.go.dev/os/exec#Cmd.Run

os/exec.ExitError — stdlib *exec.ExitError carries ExitCode(); its .Stderr field is ONLY populated when cmd.Stderr==nil — since we attach our own buffer we MUST read our buffer, not ee.Stderr. https://pkg.go.dev/os/exec#ExitError
errors.As — errors.As(err, &ee) with ee=*exec.ExitError distinguishes a non-zero exit (→ typed *git.ExitError) from a start failure (→ wrapped generic error). https://pkg.go.dev/errors#As

Research notes (verified git behaviors table, os/exec API facts, ExitError naming-collision gotcha, dependency boundaries, env/syscall posture): plan/001_f1f80943ac34/prps/research/P1_M3_T1_S1_research.md
